title: Raspberry frangipane tart
subtitle: Almond cream tart with fresh raspberries
excerpt: A round shortcrust pastry tart filled with almond frangipane and studded with fresh raspberries. The frangipane rises around the fruit as it bakes, setting firm with a slightly crusty top.
sourceNotes: Frangipane tarts are a staple of French pâtisserie, documented in domestic cookery throughout the twentieth century. The raspberry version is a British domestic adaptation, using fresh raspberries in place of poached pears or apricots. This version follows the standard shortcrust pastry and almond cream formula.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps for 2 days at room temperature. Best eaten the day it is made.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — pastry blind-baked first at 190°C fan

glossaryTerms.definition: Shortcrust pastry shrinks during baking as the gluten contracts with heat. To minimise it: use cold butter, handle the dough as little as possible, rest it in the fridge before rolling, and chill the lined tin before blind-baking. Overhang the pastry slightly above the tin edge — the pastry will pull back, and the overhang compensates.

content.text: The pastry for this tart is a sweet shortcrust: cold butter rubbed into flour and icing sugar, enriched with an egg yolk. The two enemies of shortcrust are warmth (which softens the fat and collapses the structure) and overworking (which builds gluten and causes shrinkage). Chill the dough, chill the lined tin, and take note of

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} (for the pastry) into the {{plain-flour}} (for the pastry), {{icing-sugar}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until the mixture resembles coarse sand. Mix in the egg yolk and 1–2 tablespoons of cold water until the dough just comes together. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 190°C fan. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick and line a 23 cm tart tin with a slight overhang above the rim. Prick the base, line with baking paper, and fill with baking beans. Bake for 12 minutes. Remove the beans and paper and bake for a further 5–6 minutes until the base is dry and lightly golden. Reduce the oven to 170°C fan.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} (for the filling) and {{caster-sugar}} together until pale. Beat in the {{free-range-eggs}} from the filling and the {{almond-extract}}. Fold in the {{ground-almonds}} and {{plain-flour}} (for the filling). Spread the frangipane into the pastry case. Press the {{raspberries}} into the surface in a single layer.

Bake at 170°C fan for 35–40 minutes until the frangipane is set, golden, and a skewer in the centre comes out clean. The raspberries will sink slightly into the filling. Cool in the tin for 20 minutes before slicing.
